author: osamaaddnan6

author: jtrevorchapman
text:
@osamaaddnan6 @polybacktest Sure, but Read the existing comments And anything unclear I’m happy to clarify. This particular strategy is the simplest of my scripts. 

For backtesting though you will need to enrich the data AND establish the day (i believe it was feb 18) that polybacktest started recording

author: ganggantim
text:
@jtrevorchapman @polybacktest Hi Trevor,
Firstly, thanks so much for your continuous sharing!
I’m wondering whether the indicators (RHR, sigma, obi, etc., used in the previous GTC ladder) are still applied in the current new strategy, or which of them are still in use.
Thanks again!

author: jtrevorchapman
text:
@ganggantim @polybacktest This safe zone strategy does not use any metrics for the base strategy other than: price to beat, deltas from ptb to chainlink and binance, and there delta between binance and chainlink. 
There is a second layer that adds CVD… if CVD disagrees with the directional position (up

author: 0xDaPeste
text:
@jtrevorchapman @polybacktest Hey Trevor!

Trying to understand your Nat Delta and Volatility Regime. 

What exactly is Nat Delta and how do you calculate it? And the Volatility Regime ($0-$7, $7-$15,
etc.) — is that just bucketing the Nat Delta value, or is it derived from something else? Thank you <3

author: JRdgzP

author: leo_guimaraes49
text: @jtrevorchapman @polybacktest What is the exact CVD/confidence overlay rule on top of your base signal: how do you compute CVD (data source + window), what threshold defines disagreement, and when does it ignore a trade vs dampen/amplify position size?
